page: Amazon Web Services c7i.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1926.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 32.73 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Text processing performance is highly competitive, scoring 2743.0 in Geekbench, which ranks in the top 10%. Multi-core compute metrics, such as the Passmark CPU score of 13119.77 and stress-ng multi-core performance of 7550.03, sit within the average range. However, memory subsystem performance shows some limitations, with the Passmark memory score falling into the bottom 25% tier, and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth measured at 47.98 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i.2xlarge offers a specialized compute-to-memory ratio designed for CPU-bound tasks without the overhead of unused RAM or local storage. The inclusion of advanced instruction sets, such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, enhances its capability for specific vector and matrix operations. This instance is suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory, a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by top-tier text processing and high-ranking single-threaded gzip compression. While multi-core compute and database operations perform at average levels, memory subsystem benchmarks indicate some performance limitations. Given its high-compute, low-RAM profile, this instance offers a cost-effective solution for CPU-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is suited for web serving, software compilation, text processing, and moderate database tasks.
